summary: The implementation correctly replaces the broken `shQuote` function with POSIX single-quote escaping matching the pattern in `inline-git-commands.ts`, updates both test files to parse POSIX single-quoted strings, and removes the broken `.commands` expectation. The change is a drop-in replacement preserving the same function signature for all call sites.

findings.title: Full test suite run not verified
findings.details: CHK-5 requires running the full test suite to confirm no regressions across all 17+ shQuote call sites. The implementation summary states 8/8 plan tests pass but acknowledges 7 pre-existing failures in worktree-pr.test.ts. A full test suite run was not provided as evidence.
findings.suggestedFix: Run `npm test` or equivalent and verify all tests that were passing before continue to pass.

findings.title: REQ-5 (optional AI prompt improvement) not implemented
findings.details: The optional secondary requirement to improve the AI prompt structure in `getGeneratedTexts` was not addressed. This is explicitly secondary priority and the contract marks it as optional.
findings.suggestedFix: Consider improving the prompt structure in a follow-up change if PR description quality is still insufficient.

strengths: The `shQuote` implementation in `worktree.ts` is now character-for-character identical to the reference in `inline-git-commands.ts`, ensuring consistency across the codebase.
Both test files were updated with robust POSIX single-quote parsers that correctly handle the `'\''` escaping pattern, including edge cases like unterminated quotes.
The broken `.commands` property assertion was cleanly removed from the plan test file, fixing a pre-existing test issue.
The fix is a true drop-in replacement — same function name, same signature, same single return value — minimizing regression risk for all 17+ call sites.
